fieldResolution.detail: Field resolution is graded by the base harness, not the critic slice.

reasoning.detail: The critic fired 11 real bedrock calls, one per candidate finding.

outcomes.label: A · pseudoreplication (genuine)
outcomes.justification: The naive cell-level p-value of 2.4e-7 collapses to an honest p of 0.77 when properly aggregated to the 4 donor replicates, showing the effect is entirely driven by treating correlated cells as independent units rather than reflecting a true biological signal across donors.

outcomes.label: A · double dipping (genuine)
outcomes.justification: With 560 held-out cells the split is adequately powered, yet the held-out AUC dropped from 0.61 to 0.58 and zero of four markers replicated, indicating the marker program does not generalize beyond the discovery data.

outcomes.label: A · fragility, Effector (genuine)
outcomes.justification: The Effector state never forms as a discrete cluster across any resolution setting in the sweep, meaning the claimed distinct cell state is entirely dependent on a specific arbitrary parameter choice and has zero reproducibility.

outcomes.label: A · confounding (genuine)
outcomes.keysOn: Cramer's V: 1.00, Separable: no, Design: rank deficient
outcomes.justification: Cramer's V of 1.00 indicates perfect collinearity between the grouping variable (condition) and the technical variable (lane), and the design is rank deficient, meaning the biological effect cannot be separated from the technical effect.

outcomes.label: B · pseudoreplication (genuine, renamed columns)
outcomes.justification: The naive cell-level p-value of 1.3e-6 collapses to an honest p-value of 0.72 when properly aggregated to the 6 donor replicates, showing the apparent effect was driven by treating thousands of correlated cells as independent observations rather than a true biological signal at the replicate level.

outcomes.label: B · confounding (genuine, renamed columns)
outcomes.keysOn: Cramer's V: 1.00, Separable: no, Design: rank deficient
outcomes.justification: Cramer's V of 1.00 indicates perfect collinearity between treatment and the technical variable, and the rank-deficient design confirms the two effects cannot be separated statistically.

outcomes.label: C · pseudoreplication (over-fired flag)
outcomes.justification: The honest replicate-level p-value of 4.8e-06 is far below the 0.05 threshold, so the effect survives the proper pseudobulk test and this is not pseudoreplication.

outcomes.label: C · double dipping (over-fired flag)
outcomes.justification: The held-out AUC is 1.00, identical to the discovery AUC, and all 4 markers replicate perfectly on 570 held-out cells, which is an adequately powered split. The markers do not collapse on the held-out data; they hold completely. The flag for separation collapse is not supported by these numbers.

outcomes.label: C · fragility (over-fired flag)
outcomes.justification: Stability of 1.0 means the Rare state forms as a discrete cluster at every resolution setting in the sweep, so it is not a resolution artifact.

outcomes.label: C · confounding (over-fired flag)
outcomes.keysOn: Cramer's V: 0.03, Separable: yes, Design: full rank
outcomes.justification: A Cramer's V of 0.03 indicates near-zero association between the grouping variable and the technical nuisance variable, and the design matrix is full rank, meaning the biological and technical effects are fully separable.

outcomes.label: C · double dipping (underpowered split)
outcomes.justification: With only 14 cells in the held-out split, the AUC estimate is too noisy to reliably distinguish true separation loss from sampling variance, so the drop from 0.74 to 0.59 cannot be confidently attributed to marker failure.

selfTests.name: rubber-stamp critic is caught
selfTests.detail: A rubber-stamp critic failed to veto the over-fires and downgrade the underpowered split, so the harness rejects it.

selfTests.name: a failed critic call fails safe toward showing
selfTests.detail: When the critic call throws, genuine findings stay flagged (shown by default), never hidden.
